behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; mocking manifest parsing/import can miss accidental early import. REAL-INFRA: requires real filesystem plugin package loading, but no subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: yes; mocked import path could always pass and not catch unintended execution. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid plugin (manifest missing a required field) whose entry module has a top-level side effect (e.g., writes a marker file or toggles shared state). Run the real loader against that directory. Assert validation error is reported for that plugin and assert the side-effect never occurred, proving no plugin code executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; pure unit test of topo-sort alone misses wiring between resolver and hook invocation. REAL-INFRA: local manifests/modules needed, not external systems. MOCK-HIDE: yes; mocked hook scheduler may hide real ordering bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp plugin dir, create plugin B and plugin A (A depends on B). Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory array/file. Run loader end-to-end and assert observed order is ["B","A"].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; isolated cycle-detector unit test can miss that loader still imports/loads one plugin. REAL-INFRA: needs real plugin manifests and load pipeline, no subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: yes; mocking import/onLoad may hide accidental partial load. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A; both entry modules and onLoad hooks emit side effects if run. Execute loader and assert cycle error includes A/B edge, and assert neither entry side effects nor onLoad side effects occur.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; mocking module import can miss Node import/execution ordering bugs. REAL-INFRA: depends on real module loading semantics and local filesystem. MOCK-HIDE: yes; mocked importer typically returns already-initialized exports. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets a global/module-scoped flag at top level and exports onLoad that asserts the flag is already set; if not, it throws. Run loader with real import path. Test passes only if import side effect happens before onLoad invocation.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; unit tests of error handler or graph logic alone miss interaction between failure propagation and actual hook execution loop. REAL-INFRA: needs real plugin set and dependency graph execution, no external APIs. MOCK-HIDE: yes; mocks may not reproduce thrown timing/state effects. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F (throws in onLoad), D (depends on F), and C (independent). Run loader. Assert F failure is reported, D is skipped/not loaded due to dependency, and C onLoad still runs successfully.
